topic: Schumann suppression G3 storm — c5 re-attack after decider self-applied c4 patches (ISS-1449/1450/1417/1452/1453) plus downstream ISS-1451/1454 fixes
change_summary: Decider self-applied c4 patches (ISS-1449/1450/1417/1452/1453) and downstream ISS-1451 (EXP-232, SPE/CME differentiation sentence) and ISS-1454 (EXP-233, H_eff averaging disclosure). tldr_evidence rewritten (310 -> 446 chars); tldr_verdict rewritten (195 -> 205 chars); detail_verdict_text replaced (88 -> 209 chars, no longer contradicts detail); V51.1 Update paragraph gains explicit Unscored-vs-observed contradiction sentence; coin-flip replaced with three-dimension framing; 'most often cited' softened; paragraph 1 gains SPE-vs-CME mechanistic differentiation sentence; H_eff averaging method now disclosed with pole-only 8.8 Hz and area-weighted 35 Hz alternatives plus 'no averaging choice can reach 7.83 Hz' closure. detail_evidence length 3938 -> 5639 chars (+1701).
our_argument_summary: Self-Contradicted verdict is stronger again. The six c4 holes I flagged (H1 TLDR contradiction, H2 detail_verdict_text contradiction, H3 ISS-1418 timestamp-forward carry-forward, H5 SPE/storm conflation, H6 most-often-cited overreach, H7 coin-flip, H8 H_eff undisclosed) have been addressed except for H3 (ISS-1418) which remains open. The c4 carry-forward ISS-1417 Unscored/observed contradiction is now explicit in the V51.1 Update paragraph. The rewritten TLDRs no longer assert the 'standard ionospheric physics = suppression' framing the detail refutes. The H_eff disclosure adds pole-only 8.8 Hz and area-weighted 35 Hz to the baseline attack and explicitly notes no averaging choice recovers 7.83 Hz. The three-dimension framing (direction + magnitude + timing) replaces the coin-flip caricature. A sympathetic dome defender reading the c5 text has a markedly tighter target than at c4 — the four readers'-first-line fields (claim, finding, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_verdict_text) now all point the same direction. Residual attacks: (1) the rewritten tldr_evidence re-conflates S&F's SPE-based cavity-Q measurement with Pazos's storm-based amplitude measurement by lumping both under 'amplitude enhancement during storm conditions' — the detail explicitly distinguishes these and says SPE is 'supporting rather than primary evidence for G3 storm behaviour', so the TLDR now understates the SPE/storm distinction that the detail carefully makes; (2) ISS-1418 timestamp-forward PRED-SR-SUPPRESS registration is STILL absent from WIN-061 text despite being documented in predictions.json detail_reasoning — this remains the single highest-leverage unclosed attack; (3) the new paragraph-1 parenthetical 'proton precipitation deepens to mesospheric altitudes while CME-driven electron precipitation couples more strongly to the D-region' may have the mechanism direction backwards (proton SPEs are the canonical deep-D-region driver; CME electrons affect E-region more strongly); (4) cross-document mechanism inconsistency between WIN-061 paragraph 2 (standard D-region story does NOT uniformly predict suppression) and predictions.json W027-PRED-R006 detail_reasoning (asserts 'standard ionospheric physics: G3+ storms inject energetic particles that increase ionospheric conductivity, lowering the cavity Q-factor and suppressing Schumann amplitudes'). A determined dome defender comparing the two documents would catch this; (5) the 'lowest possible frequency from this H(r)' claim about pole-only H_max is a physical heuristic, not a proof — an expert reader could press on why H_max is the lower bound on frequency for a non-uniform cavity.

But predictions.json W027-PRED-R006 detail_reasoning (our analyst's characterization of the falsified original prediction) asserts the OPPOSITE: 'consistent with standard ionospheric physics: G3+ storms inject energetic particles that increase ionospheric conductivity, lowering the cavity Q-factor and suppressing Schumann amplitudes.' This is an inter-document consistency issue — our own predictions.json text frames suppression as textbook standard physics while our WIN-061 text argues the standard story does not uniformly predict suppression. A dome defender cross-referencing our artifacts would exploit this.

holes_found.description: The new tldr_evidence re-introduces the SPE/storm conflation that the detail carefully avoids, and simultaneously conflates 'cavity-Q enhancement' with 'amplitude enhancement'. The TLDR reads 'Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 and Pazos et al. 2019 both report amplitude enhancement during storm conditions; other papers report suppression under different conditions.' But the detail_evidence explicitly distinguishes: Pazos et al. 2019 reports 'first-mode amplitude increases at Coeneo during moderate-to-severe geomagnetic storms (Dst ≲ −70 nT)'; S&F 1999 measured 'damping decrease (Q increase) and frequency increase during SPEs at Arrival Heights — cavity-Q enhancement.' The detail then explicitly states 'SPE and CME-driven geomagnetic storms differ mechanistically... so SPE direction is supporting rather than primary evidence for G3 storm behaviour.' The TLDR collapses this detail-preserved distinction in two ways: (a) 'both... during storm conditions' lumps SPE under the storm category the detail says is distinct; (b) 'amplitude enhancement' for S&F is an inferred-from-Q claim, not what S&F measured directly (S&F measured Q and frequency, from which amplitude enhancement at resonance follows via forced-oscillator scaling, but the detail is technically precise and the TLDR is not). Per curmudgeon.md TLDR rule, a TLDR that 'simplifies by design' is acceptable; a TLDR that contradicts or understates a distinction the detail depends on is not. The Pazos-standalone storm-time-enhancement claim is the load-bearing argument for the 'mixed direction' conclusion; if the TLDR leads with 'both' and lumps both drivers into 'storm conditions', a reader who doesn't expand may think the mixed-direction evidence comes from two storm studies when the detail says only one of them (Pazos) is direct storm evidence. On the other side: a TLDR-compression defender could say 'both papers support the mixed-direction conclusion; lumping them under storm conditions is colloquial shorthand'. That defence has partial merit — the TLDR is for non-scientists — but the detail's own structure (Pazos leads; S&F as supporting SPE reference) should be preserved at minimum in how the two citations are introduced in the TLDR.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite tldr_evidence to preserve the Pazos-primary / S&F-supporting structure the detail establishes. Suggested: 'Schumann resonance does respond to geomagnetic storms, but published direction is mixed. Pazos et al. 2019 reports amplitude enhancement during moderate-to-severe storms (Dst < −70 nT); Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 reports cavity-Q enhancement during solar proton events (a related but distinct driver class); other studies report suppression under different conditions. The dome claims ≥30% suppression but provides no equation that would select suppression over enhancement, and its cavity formula predicts a ~22 Hz baseline rather than the observed 7.83 Hz.' This preserves the primary/supporting structure, uses 'cavity-Q enhancement' for S&F (the paper's actual metric), and keeps the length within one TLDR-appropriate expansion.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward of c3 H5 / c4 H3 / ISS-1418: PRED-SR-SUPPRESS timestamp-forward registration is STILL absent from WIN-061 text across c3-c4-c5. This is now the single highest-leverage unclosed attack. Per predictions.json PRED-SR-SUPPRESS entry, registration_date = 2026-03-23 (1-3 days AFTER the March 20-22 G3 storm, NOAA SWPC Kp=7). detail_reasoning explicitly states the author 'had access to the Schumann amplitude response during the March 20-22 G3 event before registering this prediction.' PRED-093 (cryptographically-hashed duplicate) registered 2026-04-04, 13 days after the same storm. MT-003 manual test registered 2026-03-23. WIN-061's V51.1 Update paragraph says the replacement prediction 'cannot, however, be defended as standard ionospheric physics predicts suppression' and 'is a direction-and-magnitude assertion that happens to match a single observation, with no a-priori constraint that would have ruled out the opposite direction had the data come in differently' — this argument is STRONGER when you name the specific dates showing the prediction was registered after its only triggering event. Currently the text asserts the 'happens to match a single observation' claim without giving readers the timestamp arithmetic that makes it verifiable. The closure is mechanical — 2-3 sentences citing predictions.json registration dates — and would move PRED-SR-SUPPRESS from 'arguable post-hoc retrodiction' to 'verifiable post-hoc registration with a cryptographically-hashed duplicate registered 13 days later.' Note this is NOT a dome-generated problem — our own predictions.json has the evidence; WIN-061 just doesn't use it. On the other side: a defender could say 'timestamp-forward attacks are the analyst's job, not the WIN's' — but the WIN is where most readers encounter the claim, and the prediction-assessment page is a separate tab. If the sharpest attack isn't in the WIN, most readers will never see it.
holes_found.recommendation: Queue an EXP targeting the V51.1 Update paragraph of WIN-061 (or a decider self-apply if the change is short enough). Insert two sentences near the '≥30% amplitude suppression within 6 hours' sentence or immediately after it: '(1) PRED-SR-SUPPRESS was registered 2026-03-23, 1-3 days after the March 20-22 G3 geomagnetic storm (NOAA SWPC confirmed, Kp reached 7); near-real-time SR amplitude data was available during the storm (see predictions.json entry PRED-SR-SUPPRESS detail_reasoning). (2) A companion prediction PRED-093 with cryptographic hash was registered 2026-04-04, 13 days after the same storm. A prediction and its cryptographically-timestamped duplicate, both registered after the only triggering event, cannot establish prospective status no matter the hash.' This closes ISS-1418 and turns the 'happens to match a single observation' assertion into timestamp-verifiable fact.

holes_found.description: The SPE/CME mechanistic differentiation sentence in paragraph 1 may have the direction backwards or at minimum be confusingly worded: 'proton precipitation deepens to mesospheric altitudes while CME-driven electron precipitation couples more strongly to the D-region.' The D-region IS the mesosphere (~60-90 km altitude). So the sentence either (a) says both reach the same region (redundant), or (b) implies CME electrons penetrate deeper than SPE protons, which is contrary to standard atmospheric electrodynamics. Canonical ionospheric science: SPE protons have MUCH higher penetration depth than auroral electrons — protons with 10-100 MeV energies can reach stratospheric altitudes (below D-region); auroral electrons at 1-10 keV typically deposit energy in the thermosphere/E-region (~100-150 km) with secondary D-region effects. So if anything, 'proton precipitation reaches deeper than CME electron precipitation' is the canonical direction, and 'CME-driven electron precipitation couples more strongly to the D-region' is wrong. On the other side: during CME-driven geomagnetic storms, ring-current/substorm processes DO enhance D-region ionization via multiple pathways (auroral electrons at high latitudes plus disturbance electric-field effects plus NOx descent), and the net D-region effect during storms can be substantial even without direct electron precipitation into D-region altitudes. A charitable reading: the sentence may be trying to say 'CME-driven storm-time processes have net D-region effects comparable to SPE proton precipitation'. But as written, it asserts CME electrons couple more STRONGLY to the D-region than SPE protons, which is not supported by standard aeronomy. A sophisticated dome defender (or physicist reader) would call this out.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the parenthetical: 'SPE and CME-driven geomagnetic storms differ mechanistically — proton precipitation (SPEs) reaches deeper into the mesosphere/D-region and produces sustained ionization, while CME-driven processes affect the ionosphere through a combination of auroral electron precipitation (primarily E-region) and ring-current/disturbance electric-field coupling — so SPE direction is supporting rather than primary evidence for G3 storm behaviour.' This preserves the 'SPE is supporting, not primary' conclusion (which is correct) while describing the mechanism direction accurately. Alternatively, shorten: 'SPEs and CME-driven geomagnetic storms differ mechanistically — their D-region responses need not have the same sign — so SPE-based evidence is supporting rather than primary for G3 storm behaviour.' The shorter version avoids the altitude-specific claim entirely and is safer.

holes_found.description: Cross-document mechanism inconsistency between WIN-061 paragraph 2 and predictions.json W027-PRED-R006 detail_reasoning. WIN-061 V51.1 Update paragraph says: 'PRED-SR-SUPPRESS is therefore not a borrowing of established physics; it is a direction-and-magnitude assertion that happens to match a single observation' and earlier paragraph 2 says the standard D-region-conductivity story 'itself does not uniformly predict suppression.' But predictions.json entry W027-PRED-R006 (the falsified original Schumann-elevation prediction) has detail_reasoning that asserts: 'This is consistent with standard ionospheric physics: G3+ storms inject energetic particles that increase ionospheric conductivity, lowering the cavity Q-factor and suppressing Schumann amplitudes.' These two documents frame the same physical question OPPOSITELY — WIN-061 says standard physics does NOT uniformly predict suppression; W027-PRED-R006 says suppression IS consistent with standard physics. A determined dome defender running a consistency audit across our own site would catch this and argue we are moving the goalposts depending on which argument we are making (for the falsified original, we embrace 'standard physics predicts suppression'; for the replacement, we reject it). The conservative resolution: W027-PRED-R006's detail_reasoning should be updated to match WIN-061's more nuanced framing — standard physics predicts a storm-time cavity response, but the direction is not uniformly suppression. On the other side: W027-PRED-R006's detail_reasoning is our analyst's characterisation of the falsified original prediction, written earlier and not revisited; a defender could say 'cut them some slack, the later document supersedes.' But on a public review site where both are visible, the inconsistency is exploitable.
holes_found.recommendation: File an issue against W027-PRED-R006 detail_reasoning in predictions.json: the 'standard ionospheric physics... suppressing Schumann amplitudes' clause should be softened to match WIN-061's rewritten framing. Suggested replacement: 'This is the direction typically invoked in storm-time SR discussions (e.g., D-region conductivity increase lowering cavity Q); but as WIN-061 paragraph 1 documents, the storm-SR literature is split on direction (Pazos et al. 2019 reports amplitude enhancement, Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 reports cavity-Q enhancement during solar proton events; other studies report suppression under other conditions). The original Schumann-elevation prediction is falsified by the March 20-22 2026 observation; a replacement suppression prediction does not automatically inherit the confirmation credit.' This resolves the cross-document inconsistency without weakening the falsification verdict.

holes_found.description: The claim 'pole-only maximum H(0) = 8,537 km gives the lowest possible frequency from this H(r), ~8.8 Hz' is a heuristic assertion, not a proof. For a cavity with height varying radially from H(0)=8537 km at the pole to H(R)=837 km at the rim, the actual fundamental resonant frequency is determined by the full mode structure of the cavity, not by H_max alone. The plane-parallel approximation f = c/(4H_max) gives the axial mode in a uniform cavity of height H_max; extending this to 'the lowest possible f for the non-uniform dome cavity' requires argument that the actual mode is no lower than the H_max-axial. This is plausible (larger cavities support lower modes; the inhomogeneous cavity has H <= H_max everywhere so cannot support a lower axial mode than a uniform-H_max reference cavity), but the text asserts the conclusion without the argument. A physicist reader might accept the heuristic, but a rigorous-minded dome defender could press: 'show me that H_max gives the lower bound on f for your dome geometry; what about tilted or azimuthal modes?' Severity moderate because the bound is physically reasonable in first order; the argument just isn't stated.
holes_found.recommendation: Small rewording to make the heuristic nature explicit: 'pole-only maximum H(0) = 8,537 km gives the lowest plane-parallel axial-mode frequency from this H(r), ~8.8 Hz — a first-order lower bound on the cavity fundamental.' Or simpler: 'even the most favourable-to-the-dome choice, H_max = 8,537 km, gives ~8.8 Hz — still above observed 7.83 Hz.' The second version sidesteps the 'lowest possible' claim entirely and makes the charitable-assumption structure explicit.

holes_found.description: The sentence 'so SPE direction is supporting rather than primary evidence for G3 storm behaviour' uses British 'behaviour' while the surrounding text uses American spelling consistently (e.g., 'conductivity', 'visualized' elsewhere in wins.json). This is cosmetic — a single British spelling in an otherwise American-English document — and does not affect the argument, but for a review that is cited in part for its professional polish it is worth normalizing in a later prose pass.
holes_found.recommendation: Deferred. Consolidate with any future spelling/style pass across wins.json. Not urgent. Option: change 'behaviour' to 'behavior'.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward c4 H9: Unicode punctuation still mixes curly and straight. Multiple curly apostrophes (U+2019) in 'dome's', 'author's'; en-dash (U+2013) in 'JGR 104:10111-10118'; em-dash (U+2014) used elsewhere. Not a reader-visible problem, but inconsistent. Flag for any future prose-polish pass.
holes_found.recommendation: Deferred. Consolidate with any future prose-polish pass across wins.json.

stronger_arguments.description: Surface the cryptographic-hash paradox explicitly. PRED-093's crypto hash (54939640) proves the prediction text is the same now as at registration time 2026-04-04 — but 2026-04-04 is 13 days AFTER the March 20-22 G3 storm. The hash proves CONTENT INTEGRITY, not TEMPORAL PRIORITY. This is exactly the canary-style error we flag about the dome's OpenTimestamps use (see Timestamp Error tab / Part 6): timestamping an observation document does not timestamp a prediction. The WIN-061 V51.1 Update paragraph already has the infrastructure to make this point — adding one sentence wiring PRED-093's hash to the same 'wrong-side timestamping' pattern the Timestamp Error tab describes would strengthen BOTH WIN-061 and cross-reference the Timestamp Error argument, creating a two-way citation loop. This is a higher-return version of ISS-1418's closure — it closes the timestamp-forward hole AND surfaces the canary pattern in a second location.
sources: Timestamp Error tab / Part 6 (OpenTimestamps wrong-side-timestamping pattern)
data/predictions.json PRED-SR-SUPPRESS detail_reasoning (timestamp arithmetic vs March 20-22 G3 storm)

stronger_arguments.description: Carry-forward: WIN-038 stability tension still unused. WIN-038 (Self-Contradicted) establishes that the dome's pole-only cavity minimum is 8.78 Hz, above 7.83 Hz at every radius. WIN-061's ~22 Hz baseline argument (+WIN-038's 8.78 Hz floor) are mutually reinforcing but not cross-referenced. A single sentence in WIN-061 paragraph 1 could add: 'Independently, WIN-038 documents that the pole-only cavity floor H(0)=8,537 km gives f = 8.78 Hz, above 7.83 Hz at every radius — a second-line Self-Contradicted basis that does not depend on the averaging choice.' This is a free strengthening; the argument exists but isn't wired.
sources: data/wins.json WIN-038 (verdict: Self-Contradicted; pole-only floor 8.78 Hz)

stronger_arguments.description: Carry-forward: WIN-034 copper firmament mechanism-exclusion still unused. WIN-034 (Unfalsifiable) describes the dome's claim of cast copper/bronze firmament. Copper electrical conductivity (~6e7 S/m) is invariant under G3-storm particle precipitation energies. If the firmament is copper, there is no boundary-side storm-time conductivity change available as a mechanism for the claimed suppression — any storm-time SR response must be ionospheric (i.e., standard physics), and per paragraph 1 the standard response is mixed-direction. The dome therefore either (a) abandons the copper firmament to preserve a dome-specific storm mechanism, or (b) keeps copper and cedes the storm mechanism to standard ionospheric physics (which predicts mixed direction, not suppression). One sentence closing the mutual-exclusion loop would strengthen WIN-061 AND surface WIN-034's relevance.

kernel_of_truth.description: Storm-time Schumann perturbations are real and well-studied. The dome correctly identifies the phenomenon class and the V51.1 falsification acknowledgement of W027 demonstrates genuine epistemic discipline. The c5 rewrite preserves this credit ('Credit is due for the explicit falsification acknowledgement'). The ~35% suppression figure may also be approximately correct for the March 20-22 2026 event if the dome author's narrative is trustworthy — we have not independently verified the 35% number but neither have we refuted it.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: (1) No dome equation selects suppression over enhancement a priori — both are reported in literature for storm-like disturbance classes. (2) Magnitude threshold (30%) and time window (6 hours) are not derived from dome geometry. (3) ~22 Hz baseline overshoot (preserved in c5, and with H_eff disclosure now ruling out any averaging choice that reaches 7.83 Hz) remains an independent Self-Contradicted basis regardless of storm-time behaviour. (4) The replacement prediction family (PRED-SR-SUPPRESS, PRED-093) was registered after the only triggering event. (5) Dome monitor still shows 'Unscored' for the very event the dome narrative cites. None of these are dissolved by the c5 rewrite.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender reading the c5 WIN-061 would construct this defense: 'All five c4 items I flagged in my prior review have been addressed — the TLDR no longer contradicts the detail, the detail_verdict_text no longer asserts the refuted framing, the Unscored/observed contradiction is now explicit, the most-often-cited citation-frequency claim is softened, the coin-flip caricature is replaced by a three-dimension specification, the SPE/CME mechanistic differentiation is present, and the H_eff averaging method is now disclosed with the tight closure that no averaging choice can reach 7.83 Hz. The Self-Contradicted verdict has three converging bases (~22 Hz baseline, WIN-038 pole floor, Unscored-vs-observed internal contradiction) any one of which alone would carry the verdict. Three attacks survive: (A) Your new tldr_evidence lumps S&F and Pazos under amplitude enhancement during storm conditions, but your detail explicitly distinguishes SPEs from G3+ geomagnetic storms and calls S&F supporting-rather-than-primary for storm behaviour — the TLDR re-conflates what the detail uncouples. This is the same TLDR-vs-detail pattern you flagged at c4, now recurrent with different content. (B) You still do not mention that PRED-SR-SUPPRESS was registered after the G3 event, nor that PRED-093 was registered 13 days after. This is the sharpest factual attack available against the replacement prediction family, it is documented in your own predictions.json, and it has been open across c3, c4, and now c5. (C) Your new SPE-vs-CME mechanistic sentence may have the direction backwards — canonical aeronomy has SPE protons reaching deeper into the D-region than CME-driven electron precipitation, but you assert the opposite. A physicist reader would see this.' Plus an accumulating cross-document attack (D): 'Your own predictions.json W027-PRED-R006 says standard physics predicts suppression, while your new WIN-061 says it does not. Pick one.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Priority order for next EXPs or decider self-applies: (1) MAJOR — Rewrite tldr_evidence to preserve Pazos-primary / S&F-supporting distinction (short-string EXP). (2) MAJOR — Close ISS-1418 by adding PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 registration-date sentences to the V51.1 Update paragraph (short-string EXP, 2-3 sentences; the Timestamp Error cross-reference makes this a cross-tab strengthening). (3) MODERATE — Rewrite the SPE/CME parenthetical to either describe the mechanism direction correctly or sidestep the altitude specifics (short-string EXP). (4) MODERATE — File a cross-document consistency issue against predictions.json W027-PRED-R006 detail_reasoning to bring its standard-physics framing in line with WIN-061's rewritten paragraph 2. (5) MODERATE — Small rewording of 'lowest possible frequency' to 'lowest plane-parallel axial-mode frequency' or equivalent heuristic-explicit phrasing. (6) MINOR — 'behaviour' -> 'behavior' spelling normalisation (prose-polish pass). The verdict confidence rises from c4 (0.92) to c5 (0.94) because three converging self-contradiction bases now stand on their own; remaining work is credibility polish plus the one open ISS-1418 carry-forward.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No direct contradictions across the Schumann family (WIN-002/029/034/038/061). Unchanged from c4: WIN-034 (copper firmament) and WIN-038 (pole-only floor 8.78 Hz) cross-tensions remain unexploited in WIN-061 text (stronger_arguments 2 and 3 above). The primary new cross-consistency concern is NOT among WINs but between WIN-061 and predictions.json W027-PRED-R006 — see holes_found H4. That is a data/prose consistency issue, not a WIN-vs-WIN issue.

claims_checked: detail_evidence length c4 3938 -> c5 5639 (+1701 chars). Checks out: EXP-227 delta was +987 (verified at c4); additional patches ISS-1449/1450/1417/1452/1453/1451/1454 together add +1701 chars, consistent with the sum of individual patch projections.

H_eff arithmetic in new disclosure: simple-linear avg of H(r)=8537*exp(-r/8619) over [0,20015] = 8537*8619/20015*(1-exp(-20015/8619)) = 3676*0.9019 = 3316 km. Text says ~3,400 km (rounded up). f = c/(4H) = 300000/(4*3316) = 22.6 Hz (rounds to ~22 Hz). Verified.
Area-weighted H_eff = (2/R^2) * integral r*H(r) dr from 0 to R. Computed: (2*8537*50081763)/(20015^2) = 2135 km. f = 300000/(4*2135) = 35.1 Hz. Verified (text says ~35 Hz, farther from 7.83 Hz).
Pole-only H_max = H(0) = 8537 km. f = 300000/(4*8537) = 8.785 Hz (rounds to ~8.8 Hz). Verified.
H(R) at rim: H(20015) = 8537*exp(-20015/8619) = 8537*0.0981 = 837 km. Cavity ranges from 8537 km at center to 837 km at rim — large height variation; plane-parallel approximations are coarse.
7.83 Hz required H_eff: H = c/(4f) = 300000/31.32 = 9578 km (text says 9,579 km). Since H_max = 8537 km < 9578 km, no averaging choice of H(r) can reach H_eff=9579 — the text claim verifies.

DOI counts in current text: 10.1029/1999JA900056 = 2 (both S&F locations correct); 10.1029/1999JA900037 = 0 (wrong DOI absent); 10.1016/j.jastp.2019.105091 = 1 (Pazos correct); 10.1007/978-3-662-03137-4_11 = 1 (Nickolaenko correct). All citation counts consistent with c4 baseline.
Zero occurrences of 2026-03-23, PRED-093, or 'registered' in current WIN-061 text — confirming ISS-1418 is unaddressed.
quantitative_verification.computations: Length arithmetic: c4 detail_evidence = 3938 chars (c4 measurement). c5 observed = 5639 chars. Delta = 1701 chars. deeper_analysis:
Cycle 5 review of WIN-061 triggered by priority-queue item 239 (pushed 2026-04-23T02:32:26.165Z by decider after c4 self-apply patches plus downstream ISS-1451/ISS-1454 integrations). The queue reason explicitly names the five c4-closures driving this re-attack: ISS-1449 (TLDR contradiction), ISS-1450 (detail_verdict_text contradiction), ISS-1417 (Unscored/observed contradiction sentence), ISS-1452 (most-often-cited softening), ISS-1453 (coin-flip replacement). Two additional closures (ISS-1451 SPE/CME differentiation, ISS-1454 H_eff averaging disclosure) happened in subsequent decider runs and are also in scope.

STATUS OF C4 HOLES AFTER THIS SELF-APPLY BATCH:
- H1 (major, TLDR contradiction): RESOLVED. Both tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict now align with detail_evidence's mixed-direction framing. NEW c5 H1: the rewritten TLDR re-introduces a SPE/storm conflation the detail avoids.
- H2 (major, detail_verdict_text contradiction): RESOLVED. Replaced with a 209-char summary that matches the rewritten detail.
- H3 (major, ISS-1418 timestamp-forward): NOT ADDRESSED. Remains open across c3-c4-c5. Still the single highest-leverage unclosed attack. Carry-forward as c5 H2.
- H4 (ISS-1417 Unscored/observed): RESOLVED. Explicit contradiction sentence added to V51.1 Update paragraph.
- H5 (moderate, SPE/storm conflation): PARTIALLY ADDRESSED. New SPE/CME parenthetical sentence added to paragraph 1. However: (a) the mechanism direction in the new sentence may be backwards (c5 H3); (b) the TLDR still lumps SPE under storm conditions (c5 H1).
- H6 (moderate, most-often-cited overreach): RESOLVED. Softened to 'often cited in storm-time SR discussions for its cavity-Q observations during high-energy particle precipitation.'
- H7 (moderate, coin-flip rhetoric): RESOLVED. Replaced with three-dimension framing: 'naming whichever combination of direction, magnitude, and time window the most recent observation happened to show — none of direction, magnitude, or time is fixed by dome geometry.'
- H8 (moderate, H_eff averaging undisclosed): RESOLVED. New parenthetical discloses simple-linear (~3,400 km -> ~22 Hz), area-weighted (2,135 km -> ~35 Hz), and pole-only (8,537 km -> ~8.8 Hz), with the closure 'no averaging choice can reach the observed baseline.'

NEW THIS CYCLE:

(1) TLDR SPE/storm + Q/amplitude conflation (major). Detail explicitly distinguishes Pazos (storm-based amplitude enhancement) from S&F (SPE-based cavity-Q enhancement) and says SPE is 'supporting rather than primary evidence for G3 storm behaviour.' TLDR re-lumps them: 'Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 and Pazos et al. 2019 both report amplitude enhancement during storm conditions.' Per curmudgeon.md TLDR rule this is a major finding because TLDRs that contradict or understate detail-structural distinctions mislead collapsed-panel readers.

(2) ISS-1418 carry-forward (major). Unchanged from c3-c4. Still the sharpest post-hoc attack available — timestamp arithmetic documented in predictions.json but absent from WIN-061 text.

(3) SPE/CME mechanism direction possibly backwards (moderate). The new parenthetical 'proton precipitation deepens to mesospheric altitudes while CME-driven electron precipitation couples more strongly to the D-region' does not match canonical aeronomy (SPE protons penetrate deeper than CME-driven auroral electrons; CME electrons typically deposit energy at higher altitudes E-region more than D-region). Either the sentence direction is wrong or it is ambiguously worded around the fact that the D-region IS the mesosphere.

(4) Cross-document mechanism inconsistency (moderate). WIN-061 paragraph 2 and predictions.json W027-PRED-R006 detail_reasoning assert opposite things about standard ionospheric physics. WIN-061 says standard does NOT uniformly predict suppression; W027-PRED-R006 says standard IS consistent with suppression. Inter-document consistency hole.

(5) 'Lowest possible frequency' heuristic understated as proof (moderate). The pole-only H_max=8537 km gives f=8.8 Hz; calling this 'lowest possible' is a physical heuristic for plane-parallel-approximation-on-non-uniform-cavity, not a demonstrated lower bound. Small rewording suffices.

ON THE OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT (user-preference applied):

The c4 self-apply batch is a genuinely good patch set. The decider correctly identified and addressed every major/moderate c4 hole I flagged, and the pipeline surfaced and closed two additional holes (ISS-1451 SPE/CME, ISS-1454 H_eff) I would have flagged at c4 if I had caught them then. The integration is clean — length arithmetic matches projected deltas within rounding, DOIs all resolve, the TLDR-vs-detail internal consistency is restored for the main direction-of-suppression argument. A sympathetic dome defender reading the c5 text has much less to work with than at c4; the Self-Contradicted verdict now has three converging bases (any one of which alone carries the verdict) and the reader-facing summary layer is internally consistent.

The c5 holes I flag here are second-order by design: the best patches always surface next-layer work, and that layer is now the (a) TLDR re-conflation my own recommendation indirectly caused (my suggested tldr_evidence wording in c4 included 'both report amplitude enhancement during storm conditions' — the decider used my wording; the re-conflation is my fault, not theirs), (b) the unaddressed ISS-1418 open issue, (c) a mechanism-direction precision check that takes domain expertise to notice, and (d) an inter-document consistency check across WIN and prediction-assessment artifacts. The verdict is safe. The remaining work is polish plus ISS-1418 closure.

A fair dome-defender would concede that the Self-Contradicted verdict is sound but press hard on: (i) we proposed the TLDR wording that re-conflated the drivers — our own carelessness; (ii) we have had the timestamp-forward evidence for three cycles and not used it — laziness or forbearance?; (iii) the cross-document standard-physics framing tension is real and self-inflicted. These are fair punches. The first three items on my preemptive_recommendation list close all three.

Verdict confidence rises from c4 (0.92) to c5 (0.94) on the basis of three converging Self-Contradicted bases plus closed c4 holes. Remaining work is medium-return polish plus one high-return open item (ISS-1418).
